name: barbell jefferson squat
instructions: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, resting it on the front of your body, just below your waist.
Step your left foot forward and your right foot back, keeping your feet shoulder-width apart.
Bend your knees and lower your body down into a squat position, keeping your back straight and chest up.
Push through your heels to stand back up to the starting position.
Repeat the movement, alternating your forward and back foot with each repetition.

Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out. Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, resting it on the front of your body, just below your waist. Step your left foot forward and your right foot back, keeping your feet shoulder-width apart. Bend your knees and lower your body down into a squat position, keeping your back straight and chest up. Push through your heels to stand back up to the starting position. Repeat the movement, alternating your forward and back foot with each repetition.

conditionNotes.note: Axial loading under fatigue may worsen asymmetrical curves. Prefer split-stance or supported variations.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Loaded spinal flexion or heavy axial loading should be avoided. Use supported variations.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
